book: On the Duties of the Clergy.

On the Duties of the Clergy.
Chapter XXIII. The good faith of those who are easily bought over with money or flattery is a frail thing to trust to.

ccel.title: NPNF2-10. Ambrose: Selected Works and Letters

source.title: NPNF2-10. Ambrose: Selected Works and Letters

title: Chapter XXIII. The good faith of those who are easily bought over with money or flattery is a frail thing to trust to.

source.title: NPNF2-10. Ambrose: Selected Works and Letters

verses.book: On the Duties of the Clergy.
verses.text: Who would suppose that those who are bought over to obedience by money, or those who are allured by adulation, would ever be faithful to them?

verses.book: On the Duties of the Clergy.
verses.text: For the former are ever ready to sell themselves, whilst the latter cannot put up with a hard rule.

verses.book: On the Duties of the Clergy.
verses.text: They are easily won with a little adulation, but if one reproves them by a word, they murmur against it, they give one up, they go away with hostile feelings, they forsake one in anger.

verses.book: On the Duties of the Clergy.
verses.text: They prefer to rule rather than to obey.

verses.book: On the Duties of the Clergy.
verses.text: They think that those whom they ought to have placed over them ought to be subject to themselves, as though indebted to them by their kindness.

verses.book: On the Duties of the Clergy.
verses.text: What man is there that thinks those will be faithful to himself, whom he believes he will have to bind to himself by money or flattery?

verses.book: On the Duties of the Clergy.
verses.text: For he who takes thy money supposes that he is cheaply held, and looked down upon, unless the money is paid again and again.

verses.book: On the Duties of the Clergy.
verses.text: So he frequently expects his price; whilst the other, who is met with prayer and flattery, is always wanting to be asked.
